name: Bhai Harcharan Singh Fakkar
body: Bhai Harcharan Singh Fakkar belongs to one of the rababi families that can be traced to Nankana Sahib. They are among the rababis that moved to Patiala, Punjab during the partition. Bhai Harcharan Singh is the 4th son of Bhai Jagtar Singh Fakkar and his grandfather and granduncle, Bhai Sarmukh Singh and Gurmukh Singh Fakkar respectively, were renowned raagis in Nankana Sahib before the division of Punjab. Bhai Harcharan Singh received his training from his family, mainly his elder brother Bhai Harinder Singh, who was a student of both Ustad Bakar Hussain of Patiala Gharana and Ustad Salamat Ali Khan of Sham Chaurasi Gharana. Bhai Harinder Singh developed the current sound that defines their style and it can be recognized within their family. Unfortunately, Bhai Harcharan Singh Ji passed away some years ago in his mid 30s, leaving behind his wife and son, Bhai Lal Singh Fakkar who is very talented and holds promise of keeping the rababi tradition alive. This page is dedicated to the late Bhai Harcharan Singh Fakkar and the recordings below feature him in many samagams. His taans were phenomenal and it was a real pleasure to hear him perform with Bhai Harinder Singh. They made quite a formidable pair. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Harcharan Singh Fakkar, please contact us.
